description: Remove deprecated forwardRef wrappers from all side panel retro components, adopting the React 19 ref-as-prop pattern

userStories.title: Remove forwardRef from Button and Input retro components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Button and Input to use React 19 ref-as-prop instead of forwardRef so the code follows current React 19.2 best practices and is less verbose
acceptanceCriteria: Button component in retro/Button.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
Input component in retro/Input.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
Both components include ref in their props interface as React.Ref<ElementType>
displayName assignments are removed (no longer needed without forwardRef)
import of forwardRef is removed from both files
All existing consumers continue to work (no call-site changes needed — React 19 ref-as-prop is backwards compatible)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Remove forwardRef from Accordion and Loader retro components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Accordion and Loader to use React 19 ref-as-prop instead of forwardRef so the code follows current React 19.2 best practices
acceptanceCriteria: AccordionItem in retro/Accordion.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
AccordionContent in retro/Accordion.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
Loader in retro/Loader.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
The `import * as React from 'react'` in both files is replaced with targeted imports (only what's actually used)
displayName assignments are removed
All existing consumers continue to work
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Accordion.tsx (lines 7, 22) — two forwardRef wrappers (AccordionItem, AccordionContent)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Loader.tsx (line 31) — one forwardRef wrapper

Both files use `import * as React from 'react'` to access `React.forwardRef`, `React.ComponentRef`, `React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef`, and `React.HTMLAttributes`. After removing forwardRef:
- Replace `React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof X>` with `React.ComponentProps<typeof X>` (React 19 equivalent that includes ref)
- Or use targeted type imports: `import type { ComponentProps } from 'react'`
- Remove `React.forwardRef` and `React.ComponentRef` usage
- Replace `import * as React from 'react'` with specific named/type imports

For Accordion: AccordionItem wraps `AccordionPrimitive.Item`, AccordionContent wraps `AccordionPrimitive.Content`. The ref and className spreading pattern stays the same — just remove the forwardRef wrapper.

For Loader: The component wraps a plain div. Same migration pattern.

Pattern:
```tsx
// Before
const AccordionItem = React.forwardRef<
  React.ComponentRef<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Item>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Item>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <AccordionPrimitive.Item ref={ref} className={cn('...', className)} {...props} />
));

// After
const AccordionItem = ({ className, ref, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Item>) => (
  <AccordionPrimitive.Item ref={ref} className={cn('...', className)} {...props} />
);
```

userStories.title: Remove forwardRef from Dialog retro component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all Dialog sub-components to use React 19 ref-as-prop instead of forwardRef so the code follows current React 19.2 best practices
acceptanceCriteria: DialogOverlay in retro/Dialog.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
DialogContent in retro/Dialog.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
DialogHeader in retro/Dialog.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
DialogBody in retro/Dialog.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
DialogFooter in retro/Dialog.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
The `import * as React from 'react'` is replaced with targeted imports
All 5 displayName assignments are removed
All existing consumers continue to work
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
File to change: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Dialog.tsx

This file has 5 forwardRef wrappers (most in any single file):
- DialogOverlay (line 11) — wraps DialogPrimitive.Overlay
- DialogContent (line 26) — wraps DialogPrimitive.Content
- DialogHeader (line 48) — wraps a plain div
- DialogBody (line 66) — wraps a plain div
- DialogFooter (line 71) — wraps a plain div

For Radix primitive wrappers (Overlay, Content): Use `React.ComponentProps<typeof DialogPrimitive.X>` which includes ref in React 19.

For plain div wrappers (Header, Body, Footer): Use `React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> & { ref?: React.Ref<HTMLDivElement> }` or `React.ComponentProps<'div'>`.

The file also uses `import * as React from 'react'`. After migration, replace with targeted imports:
```tsx
import type { ComponentProps, HTMLAttributes, Ref } from 'react';
```

Keep the Object.assign compound component pattern at the bottom — only the component definitions change.

userStories.title: Remove forwardRef from Menu and Tooltip retro components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Menu and Tooltip sub-components to use React 19 ref-as-prop instead of forwardRef so the code follows current React 19.2 best practices
acceptanceCriteria: MenuContent in retro/Menu.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
MenuItem in retro/Menu.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
MenuSeparator in retro/Menu.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
TooltipContent in retro/Tooltip.tsx accepts ref as a regular prop (no forwardRef wrapper)
The `import * as React from 'react'` in both files is replaced with targeted imports
All displayName assignments are removed from both files
All existing consumers continue to work
No forwardRef usage remains anywhere in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/

npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes

userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Menu.tsx (lines 9, 24, 39) — 3 forwardRef wrappers
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Tooltip.tsx (line 29) — 1 forwardRef wrapper

Both files use `import * as React from 'react'`. Same migration pattern as previous stories.

For Menu.tsx:
- MenuContent wraps DropdownMenuPrimitive.Content
- MenuItem wraps DropdownMenuPrimitive.Item
- MenuSeparator wraps DropdownMenuPrimitive.Separator

For Tooltip.tsx: TooltipContent wraps TooltipPrimitive.Content and adds CVA variant styling.

The TooltipContent props type combines Radix props with CVA VariantProps. After removing forwardRef:
```tsx
const TooltipContent = ({
  className,
  sideOffset = 4,
  variant,
  ref,
  ...props
}: ComponentProps<typeof TooltipPrimitive.Content> & VariantProps<typeof tooltipContentVariants>) => (
  ...
);
```

This is the last story — verify no forwardRef usage remains in the side panel: `grep -r 'forwardRef' platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/` should return zero results.

This is the final story and touches browser UI, so e2eCheckpoint is true to ensure E2E tests pass after the full migration.
